prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_rf', seasonality_champion='FT_catboost', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: FT_rf emerges as the overall champion with lowest RMSE (7276) and best trend tracking (0.56 slope err), while FT_catboost leads in seasonality capture (0.364 correlation). The analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.61) and high seasonal variance (0.52), indicating that different models excel at different aspects of the series. With both early horizon specialists (rf, FT_catboost) and late horizon specialists (catboost, FT_rf) differing, DBA combination is recommended to leverage this diversity rather than relying on a single approach.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "FT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "rf", "late": "catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["FT_rf", "FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "catboost", "rf", "CWT_rf", "svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["ARIMA", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "FT_svr", "CWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.6064,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "FT_rf emerges as the overall champion with lowest RMSE (7276) and best trend tracking (0.56 slope err), while FT_catboost leads in seasonality capture (0.364 correlation). The analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.61) and high seasonal variance (0.52), indicating that different models excel at different aspects of the series. With both early horizon specialists (rf, FT_catboost) and late horizon specialists (catboost, FT_rf) differing, DBA combination is recommended to leverage this diversity rather than relying on a single approach."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "dba_combination", "ridge_stacking_l250_topk5"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong per-horizon model diversity, favoring selection methods like best_per_horizon and topk_mean. RMSE spread ratio=0.6064 shows high model dispersion (FT_rf=8084 vs DWT_catboost=13049), justifying inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts. ridge_stacking adds regularization to prevent overfitting with only 3 windows."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Score margin between top2 is only 0.0055, indicating competitive set. No redundancy or homogeneity issues detected.",

}
raw.statistician:
{
  "add_names": ["exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim1.0", "trimmed_mean_r0.10"],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has only 5 candidates with n_windows=3 (low). Universe shows exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim1.0 achieves RMSE=8999 vs current best topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 at RMSE=10004. Adding this high-performing weighted candidate improves performance. Also adding trimmed_mean_r0.10 provides baseline diversity (current set lacks baseline_mean). Current relative_spread_mean=0.152 (<0.25) so disagreement is moderate - no need for DBA/trimmed_mean with high trim. n_unique_winners=9 (≥3) supports keeping per-horizon selection methods.",
  "changes": ["added exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim1.0 from universe leaderboard (RMSE=8999 beats current best)", "added trimmed_mean_r0.10 for baseline diversity"],
  "when_good": "If RMSE spread ratio ≥0.3 or relative_spread_mean≥0.25, would add dba_combination or higher trim_ratio trimmed_mean"
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: FT_rf emerges as the overall champion with lowest RMSE (7276) and best trend tracking (0.56 slope err), while FT_catboost leads in seasonality capture (0.364 correlation). The analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.61) and high seasonal variance (0.52), indicating that different models excel at different aspects of the series. With both early horizon specialists (rf, FT_catboost) and late horizon specialists (catboost, FT_rf) differing, DBA combination is recommended to leverage this diversity rather than relying on a single approach.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong per-horizon model diversity, favoring selection methods like best_per_horizon and topk_mean. RMSE spread ratio=0.6064 shows high model dispersion (FT_rf=8084 vs DWT_catboost=13049), justifying inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts. ridge_stacking adds regularization to prevent overfitting with only 3 windows.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5). No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection or are baselines. Universe leaderboard top candidates (exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim*) have lower scores (~0.82) than current best (topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 at 0.904). Score margin between top2 is only 0.0055, indicating competitive set. No redundancy or homogeneity issues detected.

statistician.rationale: Current set has only 5 candidates with n_windows=3 (low). Universe shows exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim1.0 achieves RMSE=8999 vs current best topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 at RMSE=10004. Adding this high-performing weighted candidate improves performance. Also adding trimmed_mean_r0.10 provides baseline diversity (current set lacks baseline_mean). Current relative_spread_mean=0.152 (<0.25) so disagreement is moderate - no need for DBA/trimmed_mean with high trim. n_unique_winners=9 (≥3) supports keeping per-horizon selection methods.
changes: added exp_weighted_average_eta2.0_trim1.0 from universe leaderboard (RMSE=8999 beats current best)
statistician.when_good: If RMSE spread ratio ≥0.3 or relative_spread_mean≥0.25, would add dba_combination or higher trim_ratio trimmed_mean
